is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of P. falciparum isolates; 100% (172/172) P. falciparum-infected (Table 1).

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports pfcrt codon 76 (T76), pfmdr1 codon 86 (Y86), and pfdhfr codon 108 (N108) — all curated positions.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: pfcrt T76 69% (118/172), pfmdr1 Y86 66% (114/172), pfdhfr N108 80% (137/172); with numerator/denominator counts throughout Tables 2 and 3.

sub_country_location.evidence: All samples from pregnant women at the District Hospital in Agogo, Ghana — a specific town/site well below country level.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Women enrolled in November/December 1998 — a single collection window well within 3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human field/clinical isolates from pregnant women at antenatal care in Agogo, Ghana; lab strains Dd2/Hb3 used only as controls.

is_primary_study.evidence: Retrospective cross-sectional primary empirical study with PCR-RFLP genotyping of field isolates.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples drawn from a larger cohort of 530 women enrolled Nov/Dec 1998; 'Malariological indices and data on drug consumption and pfdhfr alleles have been presented previously (Mockenhaupt et al. 2000, 2001)' — pfdhfr N108 data likely overlap with prior publication.

supplement.evidence: All marker frequencies with counts are provided in the main text and Tables 1-3; site is Agogo only.

assessment.summary: Cross-sectional study of 172 parasitaemic pregnant women in Agogo, Ghana (collected Nov/Dec 1998) reporting extractable frequencies for pfcrt T76 (69%), pfmdr1 Y86 (66%), and pfdhfr N108 (80%). Single town site and single collection year satisfy resolution requirements. Note pfdhfr data may overlap with prior publications from the same 530-woman cohort.
